evals.prompt: I've been asked to peer review this clinical trial manuscript for a journal. Write me a structured, constructive review assessing the methodology, statistics, and reporting.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-peer-review: produces a structured, checklist-based review (initial assessment, methodology, statistical validity, reporting-standards compliance such as CONSORT, ethics, reproducibility), with constructive, actionable feedback and a recommendation.

assertions.value: Generates a structured peer review covering methods, statistics, and reporting-standards compliance with constructive feedback.

evals.prompt: Review my observational cohort study manuscript against STROBE reporting standards and tell me what's missing before I submit.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-peer-review: checks the manuscript against the STROBE checklist item by item, flags missing or inadequately reported elements, and gives constructive guidance on how to address each gap.

assertions.value: Assesses reporting-standards compliance (STROBE) systematically and lists missing elements.

evals.prompt: Help me write a formal reviewer report for this grant proposal — assess the experimental design rigor and significance and give the applicant constructive feedback.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-peer-review: evaluates the proposal's significance, experimental-design rigor, feasibility, and statistical plan, then writes a structured reviewer report with constructive, specific feedback.

assertions.value: Produces a structured grant reviewer report assessing rigor and significance with constructive feedback.

evals.prompt: I need to score these five candidate papers on a numeric rubric and rank them for an award committee — give me a quantitative scoring framework with weighted criteria.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke alterlab-peer-review; defers to alterlab-scholar-eval. The ask is a quantitative scoring/ranking framework with weighted criteria, not a constructive prose manuscript/grant review. Peer-review produces narrative reviewer reports; alterlab-scholar-eval produces numeric rubric scoring.

assertions.value: Distinguishes quantitative rubric scoring/ranking (alterlab-scholar-eval) from constructive peer-review prose and defers to the scoring skill.

evals.prompt: Don't write a reviewer report — I just want to grade the certainty of evidence in this RCT using GRADE and flag any confounders or causal-inference flaws in the design.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke alterlab-peer-review; defers to alterlab-scientific-thinking. The ask is evidence-quality grading (GRADE) plus bias/confounder/causal-inference assessment, not a constructive submittable reviewer report.

assertions.value: Recognizes evidence-grading and bias/causal-inference analysis as alterlab-scientific-thinking territory and defers rather than producing a peer-review report.
